design_philosophy.core_loop: Infiltrate enemy stronghold while avoiding detection; collect weapons, equipment, and card keys; rescue hostages for rank progression; engage in combat when discovered or confront bosses
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Stealth vs. combat—player must decide whether to avoid guards or engage; discovery triggers escalating alert phases requiring enemy elimination or evasion
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Tactical operative conducting covert military missions with varied loadouts across diverse environments (jungle, warehouse, cargo ship, train, base)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unconventional sequel developed without series creator Hideo Kojima; emphasizes hybrid stealth-action gameplay with mandatory side-view platforming sections; includes underwater oxygen management and truth gas interrogation mechanics absent from original; reintroduces Metal Gear as mass-produced threat rather than singular weapon

difficulty.checkpointing: Floor/area-based checkpointing implied by multi-stage infiltration structure with distinct zones (jungle base, cargo ship, main base)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Rank system tied to hostage rescues and interrogations; player can choose stealth evasion vs. direct combat engagement; alert phase can be escaped by moving to adjacent screen if single exclamation mark appears

progression.upgrade_system: Rank advancement (up to six stars) based on rescued hostages and successful officer interrogations; rank determines maximum health and carrying capacity; new weapons and equipment collected throughout infiltration
progression.unlock_mechanism: Card keys unlock new areas; progression through distinct environmental zones (jungle → warehouse → cargo ship → main base); side-view platforming sections serve as mandatory progression gates
replayability_hooks: Rank-based health/capacity scaling encourages replay for higher ranks
Multiple weapon/equipment combinations available for different playstyles
Alert phase mechanics create variable encounter outcomes
Hostage rescue variation influences mission progression speed

world_building_techniques: Transceiver dialogue with three named contacts (John, Nick, Jennifer) providing mission briefing and plot revelations
Environmental progression through geographically distinct military zones suggesting escalating conflict scope
Plot twist reveal of Big Boss survival and cybernetic enhancement during final confrontation
Codename 'Operation 747' framing mission as official military campaign
Post-game UN declaration of 'World Peace Day' and casualty aftermath (John MIA, Nick posthumous promotions) providing narrative closure

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters serve as zone culminations representing enemy escalation; include unique threats (impostor John, Big Boss prototype encounter, final cybernetic Big Boss confrontation)
notable_bosses: Impostor (fake John Turner) - guard room encounter during base infiltration
Big Boss (cybernetically enhanced) - final boss revealing series continuity and stakes of weapon destruction mission

faction_indicators: FOXHOUND as player faction (Snake's elite unit)
Unnamed hostile Middle Eastern nation as adversary
United Nations as geopolitical authority validating mission success
Navy Intelligence and Marine Corps affiliations of team members (John Turner, Nick Myer)
danger_signals: Exclamation mark alert system (! = single guard awareness, !! = full alert escalation)
Alert phase triggering enemy soldier reinforcement and attack sequences
Metal Gear 2 prototype and mass-produced Metal Gear tanks representing technological threat
Nuclear weapon launch threat announced by enemy commander
Cybernetic enhancement of Big Boss indicating technological arms race escalation
environmental_storytelling: Jungle base setting suggesting remote military installation in hostile territory
Cargo ship interior with ammunition cache destruction enabling escape sequence
Warehouse and train transport logistics indicating weapon distribution network
Main base architecture with commander's lair suggesting hierarchical military structure
Storage facility housing Metal Gear 2 prototype as mission-critical objective

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor) - Snake's Revenge article
